Problem Statement: For the following exercises, each set of parametric equations represents a line. Without eliminating the parameter, find the slope of each line.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 3 + t , & y = 1 - t\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, each set of parametric equations represents a line. Without eliminating the parameter, find the slope of each line.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 8 + 2 t , & y = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, each set of parametric equations represents a line. Without eliminating the parameter, find the slope of each line.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 4 - 3 t , & y = -2 + 6 t\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, each set of parametric equations represents a line. Without eliminating the parameter, find the slope of each line.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = -5 t + 7 , & y = 3 t - 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine the slope of the tangent line, then find the equation of the tangent line at the given value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 3 \text{sin} t , & y = 3 \text{cos} t , t = \frac{\pi}{4}\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine the slope of the tangent line, then find the equation of the tangent line at the given value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \text{cos} t , & y = 8 \text{sin} t ,\end{matrix}\right . t = \frac{\pi}{2}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine the slope of the tangent line, then find the equation of the tangent line at the given value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 2 t , & y = t^{3} , t = -1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine the slope of the tangent line, then find the equation of the tangent line at the given value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t + \frac{1}{t} , & y = t - \frac{1}{t} , t = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine the slope of the tangent line, then find the equation of the tangent line at the given value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \sqrt{t} , & y = 2 t , t = 4\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find all points on the curve that have the given slope.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 4 \text{cos} t , & y = 4 \text{sin} t ,\end{matrix}\right .$$ slope = 0.5

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find all points on the curve that have the given slope.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 2 \text{cos} t , & y = 8 \text{sin} t , \text{slope} = -1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find all points on the curve that have the given slope.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t + \frac{1}{t} , & y = t - \frac{1}{t} , \text{slope} = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find all points on the curve that have the given slope.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 2 + \sqrt{t} , & y = 2 - 4 t , \text{slope} = 0\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = e^{\sqrt{t}} , & y = 1 - \text{ln} t^{2} , t = 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t \text{ln} t , & y = \text{sin}^{2} t ,\end{matrix}\right . t = \frac{\pi}{4}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = e^{t} , & y = ( t - 1 )^{2} , \text{at} ( 1 , 1 )\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: For $$x = \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , y = 2 \text{sin} t$$ where $$0 \le t < 2 \pi .$$ Find all values of t at which a horizontal tangent line exists.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: For $$x = \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , y = 2 \text{sin} t$$ where $$0 \le t < 2 \pi .$$ Find all values of t at which a vertical tangent line exists.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find all points on the curve $$x = 4 \text{sin} ( t ) , y = 4 \text{cos} ( t )$$ that have the slope of $$0.5$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find $$\frac{d y}{d x}$$ for $$x = \text{sin} ( t ) , y = \text{cos} ( t ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find the equation of the tangent line to $$x = \text{sin} ( t ) , y = \text{cos} ( t )$$ at $$t = \frac{\pi}{4} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: For the curve $$x = 4 t , y = 3 t - 2 ,$$ find the slope and concavity of the curve at $$t = 3 .$$
Answer: $$\frac{d y}{d x} = \frac{3}{4}$$ and $$\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}} = 0 ,$$ so the curve is neither concave up nor concave down at $$t = 3 .$$ Therefore the graph is linear and has a constant slope but no concavity.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: For the parametric curve whose equation is $$x = 4 \text{cos} \theta , y = 4 \text{sin} \theta ,$$ find the slope and concavity of the curve at $$\theta = \frac{\pi}{4} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find the slope and concavity for the curve whose equation is $$x = 2 + \text{sec} \theta , y = 1 + 2 \text{tan} \theta$$ at $$\theta = \frac{\pi}{6} .$$
Answer: $$\frac{d y}{d x} = 4 , \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}} = -6 \sqrt{3} ;$$ the curve is concave down at $$\theta = \frac{\pi}{6} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find all points on the curve $$x = t + 4 , y = t^{3} - 3 t$$ at which there are vertical and horizontal tangents.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, write the equation of the tangent line in Cartesian coordinates for the given parameter t .
Question: Find all points on the curve $$x = \text{sec} \theta , y = \text{tan} \theta$$ at which horizontal and vertical tangents exist.
Answer: No horizontal tangents. Vertical tangents at $$( 1 , 0 ) , ( -1 , 0 ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2} .$$
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t^{4} - 1 , & y = t - t^{2}\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2} .$$
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \text{sin} ( \pi t ) , & y = \text{cos} ( \pi t )\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2} .$$
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} , & y = t\end{matrix}\right . e^{2 t}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find points on the curve at which tangent line is horizontal or vertical.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = t ( t^{2} - 3 ) , & y = 3 ( t^{2} - 3 )\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find points on the curve at which tangent line is horizontal or vertical.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \frac{3 t}{1 + t^{3}} , & y = \frac{3 t^{2}}{1 + t^{3}}\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d y \text{/} d x$$ at the value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \text{cos} t , & y = \text{sin} t , t = \frac{3 \pi}{4}\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d y \text{/} d x$$ at the value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = \sqrt{t} , & y = 2 t + 4 , t = 9\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d y \text{/} d x$$ at the value of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aa}x = 4 \text{cos} ( 2 \pi s ) , & y = 3 \text{sin}\end{matrix}\right . ( 2 \pi s ) , s = - \frac{1}{4}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = \frac{1}{2} t^{2} , & y = \frac{1}{3} t^{3} , & t = 2\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: $$x = \sqrt{t} , y = 2 t + 4 , t = 1$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Find t intervals on which the curve $$x = 3 t^{2} , y = t^{3} - t$$ is concave up as well as concave down.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Determine the concavity of the curve $$x = 2 t + \text{ln} t , y = 2 t - \text{ln} t .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Sketch and find the area under one arch of the cycloid $$x = r ( \theta - \text{sin} \theta ) , y = r ( 1 - \text{cos} \theta ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Find the area bounded by the curve $$x = \text{cos} t , y = e^{t} , 0 \le t \le \frac{\pi}{2}$$ and the lines $$y = 1$$ and $$x = 0 .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Find the area enclosed by the ellipse $$x = a \text{cos} \theta , y = b \text{sin} \theta , 0 \le \theta < 2 \pi .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find $$d^{2} y \text{/} d x^{2}$$ at the given point without eliminating the parameter.
Question: Find the area of the region bounded by $$x = 2 \text{sin}^{2} \theta , y = 2 \text{sin}^{2} \theta \text{tan} \theta ,$$ for $$0 \le \theta \le \frac{\pi}{2} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the regions bounded by the parametric curves and the indicated values of the parameter.
Question: $$x = 2 \text{cot} \theta , y = 2 \text{sin}^{2} \theta , 0 \le \theta \le \pi$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the regions bounded by the parametric curves and the indicated values of the parameter.
Question: [T] $$x = 2 a \text{cos} t - a \text{cos} ( 2 t ) , y = 2 a \text{sin} t - a \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , 0 \le t < 2 \pi$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the regions bounded by the parametric curves and the indicated values of the parameter.
Question: [T] $$x = a \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , y = b \text{sin} ( t ) , 0 \le t < 2 \pi$$ (the “hourglass”)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the regions bounded by the parametric curves and the indicated values of the parameter.
Question: [T] $$x = 2 a \text{cos} t - a \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , y = b \text{sin} t , 0 \le t < 2 \pi$$ (the “teardrop”)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$x = 4 t + 3 , y = 3 t - 2 , 0 \le t \le 2$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = \frac{1}{3} t^{3} , & y = \frac{1}{2} t^{2} , & 0 \le t \le 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = \text{cos} ( 2 t ) , & y = \text{sin} ( 2 t ) , & 0 \le t \le \frac{\pi}{2}\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = 1 + t^{2} , & y = ( 1 + t )^{3} , & 0 \le t \le 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = e^{t} \text{cos} t , & y = e^{t} \text{sin} t , & 0 \le t \le \frac{\pi}{2}\end{matrix}\right .$$ (Use a CAS for this and express the answer as a decimal rounded to three places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: $$x = a \text{cos}^{3} \theta , y = a \text{sin}^{3} \theta$$ on the interval $$[ 0 , 2 \pi )$$ (the hypocycloid)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: Find the length of one arch of the cycloid $$x = 4 ( t - \text{sin} t ) , y = 4 ( 1 - \text{cos} t ) .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: Find the distance traveled by a particle with position $$( x , y )$$ as t varies in the given time interval: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = \text{sin}^{2} t , & y = \text{cos}^{2} t , & 0 \le t \le 3 \pi\end{matrix}\right . .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: Find the length of one arch of the cycloid $$x = \theta - \text{sin} \theta , y = 1 - \text{cos} \theta .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: Show that the total length of the ellipse $$x = 4 \text{sin} \theta , y = 3 \text{cos} \theta$$ is $$L = 16 \int_{0}^{\pi \text{/} 2} \sqrt{1 - e^{2} \text{sin}^{2} \theta} d \theta ,$$ where $$e = \frac{c}{a}$$ and $$c = \sqrt{a^{2} - b^{2}} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the arc length of the curve on the indicated interval of the parameter.
Question: Find the length of the curve $$x = e^{t} - t , y = 4 e^{t \text{/} 2} , -8 \le t \le 3 .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = t^{3} , & y = t^{2} , & 0 \le t \le 1\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}x = a \text{cos}^{3} \theta , & y = a \text{sin}^{3} \theta , & 0 \le \theta \le\end{matrix}\right . \frac{\pi}{2}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: [T] Use a CAS to find the area of the surface generated by rotating $$x = t + t^{3} , y = t - \frac{1}{t^{2}} , 1 \le t \le 2$$ about the x -axis. (Answer to three decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: Find the surface area obtained by rotating $$x = 3 t^{2} , y = 2 t^{3} , 0 \le t \le 5$$ about the y -axis.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: Find the area of the surface generated by revolving $$x = t^{2} , y = 2 t , 0 \le t \le 4$$ about the x -axis.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the area of the surface obtained by rotating the given curve about the x -axis.
Question: Find the surface area generated by revolving $$x = t^{2} , y = 2 t^{2} , 0 \le t \le 1$$ about the y -axis.
